title: Sandwich ELISA plate map (384-well)
description: 384-well sandwich ELISA layout scaled from the R&D Systems DuoSet protocol: a 7-point standard curve in quadruplicate (columns 1-4), a zero standard, and the rest of the plate as samples in duplicate.

payload.description: Sandwich ELISA plate map for a 384-well plate (16 rows A-P x 24 columns), scaled from the R&D Systems DuoSet recommended setup (https://www.rndsystems.com/resources/protocols/how-run-rd-systems-duoset-elisa). Columns 1-4 hold a 7-point, 2-fold serial-dilution standard curve in quadruplicate (highest standard in row A, descending to row G) with the zero standard at row H. The remaining wells hold unknown samples in duplicate (columns 5-22 across all rows, plus the block below the standards in columns 1-4). Columns 23-24 are left as a blank border to limit edge effects. Assay all standards and samples in duplicate.

region_labels.notes: 7-point 2-fold serial-dilution standard curve in quadruplicate down columns 1-4. Highest standard in row A, descending to row G. Per R&D Systems DuoSet.
Reagent diluent only: the standard curve's zero point and the background reference for subtraction.
Additional unknown samples in the block below the standard curve (columns 1-4, rows I-P).
Unknown samples in duplicate across adjacent column pairs (columns 5-22): up to 144 samples. Assay every sample in duplicate.
Blank border (columns 23-24) to limit evaporation and edge effects. Use for additional samples in duplicate if needed.
